author: WSvX
content:
## Workload assessment: marginal impact

Realistic workload analysis shows this fix is unlikely to be user-perceptible:

- PK lookups (WHERE id = :id) complete in ~0.05-0.2ms each via B-tree
- Runs on background thread (ensureBackgroundThread at EventsHelper.kt:452)
- Room caches prepared statements; repeated PK lookups are effectively RAM reads
- Widget update ANR window is 10 seconds — a 16ms query cost is negligible

Realistic estimates:
- Widget (1-day, 5-15 events): saves ~2ms
- Widget (1-month, 30-80 events): saves ~12ms
- Power user (200+ events): saves ~30ms

The real win is the early-exit: skip all per-event lookups when no birthday/anniversary calendars exist (birthDayEventId == -1L && anniversaryEventId == -1L). This eliminates N queries for the majority of users.

Recommendation: downgrade priority from high to low. The fix is correct code hygiene but not a performance issue under realistic workloads. See .agent-workspace/upstream-issues/0016-fix-n1-query-events-helper.md for full analysis with citations.
